mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which increases aggregate corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positively correlated with concurrent INDPRO month-over-month changes (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8% indicates severe labor market slack that forces prolonged accommodative monetary policy, compressing the equity risk premium and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or insignificantly different from baseline when UNRATE > 8% (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously low nominal federal funds rates and moderate consumer price inflation sustain negative real interest rates, which compress equity discount rates and redirect capital from fixed income into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with anchored inflation
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly higher during joint low-FEDFUNDS and moderate-CPIAUCSL regimes compared to periods with only one condition present (interaction term t-stat < 2.0 over any 3-year window).

mechanism_narrative: During post-crisis recovery regimes, month-over-month increases in housing starts signal accelerating construction activity and household formation, which boosts cyclical earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns following month-over-month HOUST increases are not positive in more than 55% of instances during post-crisis recovery periods over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth exceeding 150 thousand signals labor market momentum that supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero when PAYEMS monthly change exceeds 150 thousand (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation accelerating faster than 0.2% per month erodes corporate profit margins and prompts tighter monetary policy expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered months when CPIAUCSL monthly change exceeds 0.2% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25% reflects a zero lower bound monetary regime where forward guidance suppresses short-term interest rate volatility and induces a portfolio rebalancing effect into equities, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or insignificantly different from baseline when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real chained GDP indicates broad-based aggregate demand expansion, which lifts economy-wide corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns following positive GDPC1 growth quarters do not exceed baseline returns (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.
